section.heading: Loans by management companies
section.chapeau: It shall be unlawful for any registered management company to lend money or property to any person, directly or indirectly, if—

subsection.content: the investment policies of such registered company, as recited in its registration statement and reports filed under this subchapter, do not permit such a loan; or

such person controls or is under common control with such registered company; except that the provisions of this paragraph shall not apply to any loan from a registered company to a company which owns all of the outstanding securities of such registered company, except directors’ qualifying shares.

note.heading: Amendments
note.p: 1987—Subsec. (b). Pub. L. 100–181 struck out “to the extension or renewal of any such loan made prior to March 15, 1940, or” after “shall not apply”.
